    This week’s outside five sigma (#31)

    January 26, 2025 7 minute read

    Wentao

    Memories are the long term averages, and the outside five sigmas.

    • Twitter
    • GitHub
    • Google Scholar

    On this page

    • AlOx-Based EDWA
    • A 12 in wafer carrying 4 in wafers
    • “Multiuse optical quantum computer”
    • Large-Scale Robotic Fiber Switch Array
    • SMA-Actuated Hand Exoskeleton
    • Nanopillar Antireflection via Tape Residuals
    • CMOS-Compatible 3D Photonic Integration
    • Josephson Junction Energy-Speed Performance
    • BaTiO3 Microwave-Optical Transducer Airbridges
    • Flash Memory Layer Stacking

    • Optical Vibration Measurement Comparison

    AlOx-Based EDWA

    An Erbium-Doped Waveguide Amplifiers (EDWA) that you can pre order, from Aluvia Photonics, with AlOx instead of nitride, offering “high external fiber-to-fiber gain (>25 dB) and low noise figure (comparable to EDFA’s)”

    • https://aluviaphotonics.com/edwa-order

    A 12 in wafer carrying 4 in wafers

    A 12 in mama wafer carrying four 4 in baby wafers.

    • Ji2025: Full C- and L-band tunable erbium-doped integrated lasers via scalable manufacturing
    • they got good tool for erbium ion implantation

    “Multiuse optical quantum computer”

    First multiuse optical quantum computer comes to Japan! Shows an “old school” PPLN OPA module from 2021

    • (PPLN: Periodically Poled Lithium Niobate
    • OPA: Optical Parametric Amplifier)
      • Article: First Multiuse Optical Quantum Computer Comes to Japan
    • Paper: Kashiwazaki2021: Fabrication of low-loss quasi-single-mode PPLN waveguide and its application to a modularized broadband high-level squeezer
    • The actual article from Riken

    Large-Scale Robotic Fiber Switch Array

    Have you seen a robot moving a few thousand fibers at the same time?

    • G4 Network Topology Manager™ (NTM) Brochure Telescent - G4 NTM
    • Found thru Google OCS paper
    • Liu2023: Lightwave Fabrics: At-Scale Optical Circuit Switching for Datacenter and Machine Learning Systems
    • The piezo approach also looks interesting. Can’t find much info other than some vague patent and a OFC2017 paper
    • HUBER+SUHNER Polatis - All-Optical Switches - Background & Technologies
    • Dames2004: Beam steering optical switch

    SMA-Actuated Hand Exoskeleton

    Hand exoskeleton with shape memory alloy. Hmm torque per joint only 0.5 Nm? Never thought about it. (for rehabilitation)

    • Curcio2025: a design procedure for a novel concept of shape-memory-alloy-actuated finger exoskeleton

    Nanopillar Antireflection via Tape Residuals

    You could make these nanopillars by etching tape residuals, and it’s even better than doing it intentionally with “self-assembled monolayer silica colloidal crystals as templates” beautiful

    • (Fig. 1a has a wafer half clean and half with the colloidal crystals, and after the etch in Fig. 1b, the half without the crystals is darker/better. And the bottom two wafers are just with the tape on top, rinsed with IPA a bit so shit under the tape spreads out on the wafer, and then etched.)
      • Gu2025: Bioinspired broadband antireflection coatings on silicon wafers enabled by a polyimide-assisted self-masking technique

    CMOS-Compatible 3D Photonic Integration

    3d printed 5 um diameter ikea wood pin

    • Grabulosa2023: Additive 3D photonic integration that is CMOS compatible
    • Quite some madness going on in this review paper.

    Josephson Junction Energy-Speed Performance

    Is this claim accurate?

    • “Josephson junctions have the highest speed over energy quotient of any active circuit element, so there is no known method to computer faster with less energy.”
      • From a looonng review that’s likely going to IOP JPhys Photonics
      • Brunner2025: Roadmap on Neuromorphic Photonics
      • (they claim the benefit of using SNSPD etc lets you use much lower optical power and gives you a net win in energy cost)
      • guess what

    BaTiO3 Microwave-Optical Transducer Airbridges

    These are some of the fluffiest puffiest airbridges, can I poke one

    • Möhl2025: Bidirectional microwave-optical conversion using an integrated barium-titanate transducer
    • These airbridges are basically “copper interconnects” but for superconducting circuits, because dielectrics are lossy and you want as little of them involved as possible. They have been around for a while, if you are interested this is a good read (and there are many others).
    • Chen2014: Fabrication and characterization of aluminum airbridges for superconducting microwave circuits (also arxiv)

    Flash Memory Layer Stacking

    Guess what this is

    • March 2023: Intel and SK Hynix Show Two Paths for Flash Memory’s Future: Intel is densifying bits while SK Hynix is stacking layers

    Large-Scale Optical Switches

    Thread for big arrays of on-chip optical switches

    • The image above is a new one based on MEMS, they split a wide waveguide to either transmit or TIR the input beam, with a sawtooth edge to taper the light to have more tolerance for gap size & prevent stitching
    • Hu2025: Silicon photonic MEMS switches based on split waveguide crossings
    • PsiQuantum’s switch chip is also 64x64 (128x128 next), and seems to have a similar topology. But it is electro-optic so much faster and probably lower IL, but would be more annoying to calibrate (?)
    • Vertically actuated adiabatic coupler, mesh topology. Electrostatic pull-in lets them to contorl the n*n array with just 2n signals. Their 64x64 chip has the best pictures, but they have made 128x128 ones and 240x240 ones that are beyond one reticle size.
    • Seok2016: Large-scale broadband digital silicon photonic switches with vertical adiabatic couplers
    • Kwon2018: 128×128 Silicon Photonic MEMS Switch with Scalable Row/Column Addressin
    • Seok2019: Wafer-scale silicon photonic switches beyond die size limit
    • Google’s OCS is not exactly on-chip but the MEMS mirrors are on-chip. 176x176 max and they use 136x136. Very broadband and low IL.
    • They cited some old MEMS optical switch work with 1296 ports!
    • Urata2022: Mission Apollo: Landing Optical Circuit Switching at Datacenter Scale
    • Ryf2001: 1296-port MEMS transparent optical crossconnect with 2.07 petabit/s switch capacity
    • Ok I found those old MEMS mirror (this is derailing from on-chip but they are so sexy I have to). 5 dB IL and 38 dB crosstalk. No degradation after 18 billion cycles.
    • Aksyuk2003: Beam-steering micromirrors for large optical cross-connects
    • Lightmatter’s Passage.
    • (Btw this is a crazy thread, go check it out)

    Optical Vibration Measurement Comparison

    Comparison of Laser Doppler vibrometry, Digital holography, Uni-axial Digital Image Correlation (U-DIC), and Deflectometry for vibration analysis

    • O’Donoughue2023: Comparison of three full-field optical measurement techniques applied to vibration analysis
    • Classic paper on interferometric measurement of high velocities of any reflecting surface (have not read it yet)
    • Barker1972: Laser interferometer for measuring high velocities of any reflecting surface

    1770km Subsea OFDR Seismic Sensing

    Optical frequency domain reflectometry (OFDR) for distributed acoustic sensing has been around for a long time and used in many places, usually 10~100 km range. This is the first time it’s done in a 1770 km long subsea cable, and they could detect different seismic waves arriving along different trajectories.

    • Mazur2024: Global Seismic Monitoring using Operational Subsea Cable
    • Another example for DAS
    • This is a recent paper from David Neilson, I was checking out his work because of some old MEMS work they did in the early 2000s (and that’s because I was doing a thread about optical switch chips yesterday)

    Cryogenic Heat Capacity

    Fun fact, heat capacity of most materials goes as T^(1~3), so it’s much easier to heat things up (unintentionally) at low temperatures. Every time you switch those blue shiny Radiall microwave switches, it could heat up stuff at the 10 mK MXC plate more than 10x, and >30 min wait to cool back down.

    • Scalable Error-Correction Signals Forthcoming Efficiency Gains for Quantum Compute

    Communications Technology Exponential Growth Trends

    “Obviously, all exponential growth will eventually saturate, but many examples related to information and communications technologies have shown that exponential growth (as well as exponential energy reduction) can be maintained for decades, and even for a century.” (and multiple-century ones in , e.g., economics)

    • Winzer2018: Fiber-optic transmission and networking: the previous 20 and the next 20 years (Invited)
    • Tucker2010: Green Optical Communications—Part I: Energy Limitations in Transport
    • This website has a lot of economics data for you to plot (although it’s a bit glitchy)
    • This is log plot of US GDP Per Capita in 2017 dollars (blue), S&P accumulated in nominal dollars from $1 invested in 1871 (red), and US Long-Term Interest Rate (orange)

    LCoS-Based Wavelength-Selective Switch

    Ultrawideband wavelength-selective switch. Different input of the fiber array is separated vertically and spread out horizontally onto the grating, and then separated horizontally by wavelengths onto the liquid crystal on silicon (LCoS). The LCoS could then add a linear phase with a “sawtooth hologram” to steer different wavelengths separately.

    • Renaudier2022: Devices and Fibers for Ultrawideband Optical Communications

    High-Density Microlens Fiber Array

    Microlens fiber array with >1000 fibers!

    • Medusa™ Fiberized Microlens Array from RAM Photonics
    • Found in paper Winzer2023: The future of communications is massively parallel
    • “All steps in the process, starting from the fiber spool are fully automated, including fiber preparation, optical alignment, laser welding, and packaging. SME knowledge has been embedded in the control algorithms, allowing the system to be loaded with raw components and
    • Pitch can be as small as 50 um, so it’s actually pretty handy.
    • The other end might be people size lol if they are the usual connectors

    LLM Hallucination Leaderboard

    (second image is the full list)

    • vectara/hallucination-leaderboard

    Long ultra-High-Q Silicon Nitride Resonators

    When your waveguide is so dense and so long your chip actually looks like a tiny CD. (6 um x 80 nm silicon nitride with oxide cladding, 10 m total length, and intrinsic Q = 340 million..)

    • Liu2024: Tunable broadband two-point-coupled ultra-high-Q visible and near-infrared photonic integrated resonators
    • youtube - UCSB OCPI Group - Scanning resonance of 674nm SiN ring resonator

    CNN-Guided Robotic Fiber Connection System

    Mobile robot with a Kinova Gen3 arm to change fiber connections. Object detection is YOLOv7 fine tuned with 40 images (expanded to 200 with cutout, add noise, and change saturation & brightness), resulting average precision ~ 0.96.

    The fiber insertion requires 0.4 mm margin, while the arm repeatability is 1 mm, so they trained a CNN to generate actions from two camera images. Success rate 100% with 100 trials and average insertion time ~ 18 s.

    • Xu2024: Automated fiber switch with path verification enabled by an AI-powered multi-task mobile robot

    Datacenter Copackaged Optics Evolution

    How datacenter switch looked like in 2019, and the plan to move to copackaged optics and coherent WDM without DSP.

    • IEEE GLOBECOM 2019 // Keynote: Daniel Blumenthal - QUIET LIGHT FOR LOW-ENERGY SCALABLE DATA CENTER INTERCONNECTS

    Tags: ALD, Benes_network, CNN, Copackaged_optics, Cryogenics, DAS, Deflectometry, DRIE, EDWA, Holography, Josephson_junction, LCoS, MEMS, Microlens, Nanofab, Nanopillar, OFDR, Photonics, PPLN, SNSPD, Vibrometry, Waveguide, WDM, YOLOv7
